title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, authentic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!
longTailKeywords: best butter chicken recipe at home

Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian favorite.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This classic recipe delivers an authentic taste experience with its perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavors, making it a beloved comfort food.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and slightly charred on the edges. The chicken does not need to be cooked through at this stage. Remove cooked chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Build the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, soaked and drained cashews, remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1 tablespoon sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. The sauce should thicken slightly.

instructions.title: Blend and Finish the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick. Pour the smooth sauce back into the pan. Stir in the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala and the crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi).

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the smooth sauce. Stir well to coat the chicken. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to finish cooking through and absorb the flavors of the sauce. Stir in the heavy cream and the last tablespoon of butter. Heat through gently for 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice. Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken!

tips: For a deeper flavor, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer it marinates, the more tender and flavorful it becomes.
Always blend the sauce until absolutely smooth. Straining the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve after blending can ensure a truly restaurant-quality silky texture.
Adjust the spice level by varying the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder. For a richer color without much heat, use more. For more heat, add a pinch of regular red chili powder.
article:
**Mastering the Art of Butter Chicken: Your Guide to Classic Murgh Makhani**

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian chicken recipe, renowned for its rich, creamy tomato gravy and succulent pieces of chicken, has transcended borders to become a global culinary sensation. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore the vibrant world of Indian cuisine, mastering the classic Butter Chicken is a rewarding endeavor. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the history, ingredients, and precise steps to create an authentic Murgh Makhani that will delight your senses.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, translates literally to "chicken with butter." Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, it was reputedly created by Kundan Lal Gujral at Moti Mahal restaurant. The dish was born out of a need to use leftover tandoori chicken, which was then simmered in a rich, velvety tomato-based sauce infused with butter, cream, and a medley of aromatic spices. The hallmark of a great Butter Chicken is its balance: a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy notes, with a luxurious mouthfeel that comes from the generous use of butter and cream. It's not overly spicy, making it a beloved choice for those new to Indian food, yet its depth of flavor satisfies even the most discerning palates. The chicken, traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled, becomes incredibly tender as it finishes cooking in the decadent sauce.

## Ingredients for Classic Butter Chicken

To create an authentic Murgh Makhani, quality ingredients are key. Here's what you'll need for a classic recipe serving 4-6 people:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1.5-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain full-fat yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes or 6-7 ripe medium tomatoes, blanched, peeled, and pureed
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/4 cup cashews, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes (optional, for extra creaminess)
*   1/2 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp ground cumin
*   1/2 tsp fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   1-2 tbsp sugar (to balance acidity)
*   1/2 tsp salt, or to taste
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (or more, to taste)
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Butter Chicken

Creating a sublime Butter Chicken involves a few distinct stages, each contributing to the final masterpiece:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Preheat your oven broiler or a grill pan to medium-high. Thread chicken onto skewers if broiling, or arrange on the grill pan. Cook until lightly charred and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. The charring adds a smoky depth similar to tandoor cooking. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt butter with oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or puree). If using cashews, blend the soaked cashews with a little water until smooth and add to the pot now. Add red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and ground cumin. Mix well and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the oil to separate slightly. This step is crucial for developing flavor.
5.  **Simmer the Sauce:** Add water or chicken broth, sugar, and salt. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for another 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
6.  **Blend (Optional but Recommended):** For an extra smooth sauce, carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Strain the sauce back into the pot through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining solids. This step elevates the texture significantly.
7.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot over low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the crushed kasoori methi (fenugreek leaves), which adds a distinctive aroma and flavor. Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Gently simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the sauce's flavors and the sauce to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.
8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and a swirl of extra cream, if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be.
*   **Char the Chicken:** While a tandoor oven is ideal, grilling or broiling the chicken until it has a slight char adds a smoky depth that's characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Cook the Tomatoes Well:** The "bhuno" process (sautéing the tomatoes until the oil separates) is essential for developing a deep, rich flavor in the sauce. Patience here pays off.
*   **Blend and Strain:** For the silkiest, most luxurious sauce, blending and then straining the tomato base through a sieve is highly recommended. It removes any fibrous bits and creates that signature smooth texture.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Butter Chicken should be a balance of sweet, tangy, and savory. Use sugar to cut the acidity of the tomatoes and add a touch of sweetness. A pinch of extra salt or a squeeze of lemon juice can brighten the flavors.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** Do not skip the kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves). Crushing them between your palms before adding releases their unique aroma and is crucial for authentic flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a complete meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with the right accompaniments:

*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy garlic naan or plain naan is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or jeera (cumin) rice provides a neutral base that complements the creamy curry.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber raita (yogurt dip) helps to balance the richness of the Murgh Makhani.
*   **Salad:** A simple side salad of sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes with a lemon dressing adds freshness.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is timeless, you can certainly adapt it to your dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Dairy-Free/Vegan:** For a dairy-free version, substitute yogurt with a plant-based alternative, butter with vegan butter, and heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream. For a vegan Butter "Chicken," use paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or chickpeas in place of chicken.
*   **Spicier Version:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce.
*   **Less Sweet:** Adjust the sugar content to your liking. Some prefer a tangier sauce.
*   **Smoked Flavor:** For an extra smoky touch, you can use the "dhungar" method: place a small bowl with a hot charcoal piece in the pot of finished curry, drizzle a little oil over the coal, and cover for a few minutes to infuse the smoke.
*   **Different Proteins:** While chicken is traditional, you can also make Butter Paneer, Butter Shrimp, or even Butter Lamb.

Embrace the journey of creating this beloved Indian classic. With attention to detail and a passion for flavor, your homemade Butter Chicken will undoubtedly become a staple in your culinary repertoire, bringing the warmth and richness of Indian cuisine right to your dining table. Enjoy the process, and savor every exquisite bite!
